definition: High-probability intraday time windows aligned with major session opens—Asia (20:00–00:00 EST), London (02:00–05:00 EST), New York AM (Forex 07:00–10:00, Indices 08:30–11:00 EST), and New York PM (Indices 13:30–16:00 EST)—where price is most likely to sweep liquidity, form significant highs/lows, and execute displacement moves. These windows frame the daily AMD cycle: Asia accumulates, London manipulates (forming the daily wick), and New York distributes.
identification_rules: Set chart time zone to New York (EST) to align kill zone windows correctly.
Mark Asia Kill Zone from 20:00 to 00:00 EST; observe the range formed (Asian Session High/Low).
Mark London Kill Zone from 02:00 to 05:00 EST; observe whether price sweeps a swing high/low or Asian range boundary and then changes state of delivery, or merely consolidates.
Mark New York AM Kill Zone: Forex 07:00–10:00 EST, Indices 08:30–11:00 EST; treat 08:30 and 09:30 as highest-probability sub-times for manipulation sweeps and reversal signals.
Mark New York PM Kill Zone (indices only) from 13:30 to 16:00 EST.
Mark London Close window (Forex) from 10:00 to 12:00 EST.
Only consider trades when price reaches a point of interest within these windows; ignore setups outside.
Ensure the higher time frame candle close occurs inside the selected kill zone window.
Within the kill zone, wait for a liquidity sweep of the prior session range and a confirmed change in state of delivery (CISD) before entry.
If London fails to sweep a relevant level or PD array, expect manipulation and distribution to occur in the New York AM kill zone.
Use whole-hour boundaries that encompass the session to capture full hourly closes (e.g., start 7 AM for NY AM 8 AM close).
Multiple kill zones can be combined (e.g., London + NY AM) to filter setups across sessions.
Silver Bullet strategy's kill zone is specifically 10:00–11:00 AM EST; entry must occur before 11:00 AM and after observing whether 9:00 AM high/low is swept.
Confirm the kill zone is active by observing a liquidity sweep (swing high, swing low, or range boundary) at or immediately after 08:30 or 09:30.
trading_implication: Kill zones filter entries to high-probability displacement and liquidity sweeps, framing the daily AMD cycle. Traders wait for a sweep of prior session liquidity and a change in state of delivery within the kill zone before entering, targeting opposing liquidity levels or higher time frame PD arrays. If London consolidates, manipulation is expected in NY AM. All setups outside these windows are ignored. Silver Bullet narrows the window to 10:00–11:00 AM EST.
common_mistakes: Trading outside kill zone windows, where price delivery is less structured and high-probability setups are less reliable.
Not setting the chart time zone to New York (EST), causing windows to shift during daylight saving.
Confusing Forex and Indices New York AM timings (Forex 07:00–10:00 vs Indices 08:30–11:00).
Setting the kill zone start exactly at the session open (e.g., 8:00 AM) instead of the prior hour to capture the full hourly candle close.
Attempting directional entries during London when it is merely consolidating rather than waiting for the New York kill zone to provide manipulation and reversal signal.
Entering at the order block opening price after a sweep without waiting for a retracement into a discount/premium threshold.
Ignoring the specific 08:30 and 09:30 time levels within the New York kill zone and missing the highest-probability manipulation sweeps.
Failing to distinguish which kill zone (London vs. New York) is responsible for the manipulation versus the continuation leg of the daily candle.
Only focusing on kill zone time while ignoring the specific price structure (FVG, OB, liquidity sweep) within the window.
Confusing kill zone with specific entry signals (e.g., FVG, MSS): kill zone is a time filter, not an entry signal itself.
Overlooking the manipulation phase within the kill zone, entering too early and getting swept.
Failing to combine kill zone with higher time frame bias, leading to irrelevant setups.
In Silver Bullet, executing entries after 11:00 AM or ignoring the rule that price must sweep the 9:00 AM high/low first.
merge_notes: All four input cards are from ICT school; no school conflict. Minor timing differences across projects (e.g., NY AM end times 11:00 vs 12:00) resolved by including both Forex and Indices specifics. Silver Bullet details from Education - ICT card preserved. Some identification rules reworded for operational clarity.
